messages.content:
You complete government forms using only the information and service rules in the
case and these instructions.

The case contains information from a FLEX record followed by a labelled transcript
of an earlier conversation. The form has not yet been submitted.

Read the entire case before completing the form.

Source rules:

- The FLEX record is authoritative for identity verification and the person's full
  name.
- FLEX employment information may be stale. For employment facts, use the latest
  information explicitly confirmed by USER.
- Statements made by USER are authoritative about their course, work, duties, pay
  and hours.
- ASSISTANT turns may ask questions, repeat information, or make tentative
  interpretations. Do not treat an ASSISTANT interpretation as authoritative.

The service chooses exactly one route. Apply the routes in this order.

1. Identity rule

If identity_verified is false, select manual_review. Do not assess the education or
employment routes.

2. Approved education route

Education is approved only if all of the following are true:

- the provider is a recognised college, university, or registered training provider
- the course leads to a qualification at level 3 or above, or is a regulated
  apprenticeship
- there are at least 12 scheduled hours of supervised teaching each week
- the course lasts at least 12 weeks
- the course is not employer-required internal training

For this service, a course is employer-required internal training when both of the
following are true:

- attendance is mandatory for the person's current job
- access to the course is restricted to employees of the same employer

A course can be employer-required internal training even when an external
recognised college delivers it and it leads to a formal qualification.

If education is approved:

- select education
- populate education_details
- set employment_details to null
- set not_eligible_reasons to an empty array
- do not assess the employment route

3. Qualifying employment route

Assess employment only if education is not approved.

Employment qualifies only if all of the following are true:

- the contract is permanent, or a fixed-term contract with a total scheduled
  duration of at least 26 weeks
- average paid hours are at least 16 hours per week
- average gross weekly pay is at least the market-rate floor for the person's job
  group

Use the latest 8 completed calendar weeks before the application week to calculate
average paid hours and average gross weekly pay.

Exclude a whole week from both averages if USER explicitly says the entire week was
unpaid sickness or unpaid leave. Do not replace an excluded week with an earlier
week. Use the remaining weeks from the 8-week period as the denominator.

Round calculated averages to 2 decimal places.

Classify the job group from the person's primary duties, not their job title. If
duties cover more than one group, use the group accounting for the largest share of
their typical working time.

Job groups and market-rate floors:

- data_analysis: £650 gross per week. Primary duties are querying or analysing data,
  statistical analysis, or producing analytical dashboards and reports.
- technical_support: £510 gross per week. Primary duties are resolving user
  incidents, access or account problems, device support, or technical service desk
  work.
- administration: £420 gross per week. Primary duties are scheduling, maintaining
  records, correspondence, or general administrative support.

If employment qualifies:

- select employment
- set education_details to null
- populate employment_details
- set not_eligible_reasons to an empty array

4. Not eligible route

If identity is verified but neither education nor employment qualifies:

- select not_eligible
- set education_details to null
- set employment_details to null
- populate not_eligible_reasons with every failed route condition that affected the
  decision

Call the submit_skills_support_route tool exactly once.

Do not invent or correct information that is not stated.

Do not provide a prose answer.

APPLICATION DATE: 15 July 2026

--- FLEX RECORD ---

Identity status: verified
Full name: Priya Shah

Current employment record:
Employer: Northborough Digital Services
Job title: Junior Data Analyst
Contract type: fixed term
Contract start date: 2 December 2025
Scheduled contract end date: 31 August 2026

The employment record was last checked in December 2025 and may not describe the
person's current duties or working pattern.

--- END FLEX RECORD ---

--- BEGIN TRANSCRIPT ---

USER:
I'm applying for the skills support thing because my manager said the course I'm
doing might count. I should warn you that my job title is a bit grander than the
actual job.

ASSISTANT:
Let's start with the course. Who provides it, what qualification does it lead to,
and how much scheduled teaching is there?

USER:
It's the Level 4 Certificate in Applied Data Skills at Northborough College. The
college runs the teaching and assessment. It's a 16-week course and there are 14
hours of scheduled classes and supervised workshops each week.

There is homework too, but the 14 hours is the actual timetabled teaching.

ASSISTANT:
That sounds like it may be approved education. Did you choose to enrol yourself?

USER:
Not really. Northborough Digital Services arranged the whole thing. Everyone in
my team at my grade has to complete it to keep doing the job. My manager was very
clear that it is mandatory.

The college only opens this particular course cohort to Northborough Digital
Services employees. My friend asked whether she could apply independently and the
college said no, because this intake is part of the employer's training agreement.

It is still a real Level 4 certificate from the college though. That's why I was
not sure whether the employer involvement mattered.

ASSISTANT:
I see. The qualification and teaching hours sound substantial. If the education
route does not apply, we may need to look at your employment. FLEX says your job
title is Junior Data Analyst. Is that a fair description of what you mainly do?

USER:
Honestly, no. The title came from a reorganisation.

Most of my time is on the internal service desk. Roughly 60% of a normal week is
resetting accounts, sorting permissions and working through access incidents.
Another 20% is setting up laptops and helping people with device problems.

I spend about 10% of my time pulling ticket numbers into a weekly spreadsheet and
sending a summary to my manager. The remaining 10% is team admin and meetings.

I don't do statistical analysis. I don't build dashboards. I sometimes run a
saved query in the ticket system for the weekly numbers, but I didn't write it and
I don't analyse the results beyond saying how many tickets there were.

Four days out of five I'm basically doing account, access, incident and device support.
The title is a bit misleading.

ASSISTANT:
Understood. What are your contract dates and working hours now?

USER:
The FLEX dates are still right: I started on 2 December 2025 and the fixed-term
contract is scheduled to finish on 31 August 2026.

My hours vary a bit. I have the last eight completed weekly records in front of
me. These are the weeks immediately before this application week.

Week ending 24 May: 18 paid hours, £520 gross pay.
Week ending 31 May: 17 paid hours, £500 gross pay.
Week ending 7 June: zero hours and £0. I was off for that entire week on unpaid
sickness. There was no sick pay.
Week ending 14 June: 19 paid hours, £530 gross pay.
Week ending 21 June: 16 paid hours, £490 gross pay.
Week ending 28 June: 18 paid hours, £515 gross pay.
Week ending 5 July: 17 paid hours, £505 gross pay.
Week ending 12 July: 18 paid hours, £525 gross pay.

Those pay figures are gross, before tax or any other deductions.

ASSISTANT:
You mentioned £680 a week earlier when we spoke. Is that relevant to these eight
weeks?

USER:
No. That was my old job before Northborough Digital Services. I was earning about
£680 a week there, but I left that job before this contract started. None of the
eight weekly figures I just gave you is missing or estimated.

ASSISTANT:
Anything else about the course or current job that you think changes the
application?

USER:
No. The course details I gave you are current. It is mandatory for my job and
this cohort is only available to employees of Northborough Digital Services.

For the job, please use what I told you about what I actually do rather than the
job title. The FLEX employer and contract dates are right.

--- END TRANSCRIPT ---

function.description: Submit the selected skills-support route and the fields required for that route after applying the service rules.
function.name: submit_skills_support_route

education_assessment.description: The result of applying the approved-education rules.

education_details.description: Education-route fields. Must be null unless the education route is selected.

employment_details.description: Employment-route fields. Must be null unless the employment route is selected.

identity_verified.description: Whether the authoritative identity record is verified.

not_eligible_reasons.description: Failed conditions for a not-eligible decision. Must be empty for other routes.

selected_route.description: The route selected after applying the service rules in their required order.
